events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Sir Richard Starkey, known as Ringo Starr, was born on July 7, 1940, in Liverpool, England, and rose to international fame as the drummer for The Beatles.

angles.name: Ringo's enduring beat
angles.copy: Happy 86th Birthday to the legendary Ringo Starr! From holding down the beat for The Beatles to his stellar solo career, his influence is undeniable. What's your favourite Ringo moment?

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Sir Brian Harold May, born on July 19, 1947, in Hampton Hill, Middlesex, is the iconic lead guitarist and co-founder of the rock band Queen.

angles.name: Guitar legend's birthday
angles.copy: Wishing a very Happy 79th Birthday to the one and only Dr. Brian May! From Queen's anthems to his astrophysics, he truly rocks on every level. What's your favourite Queen guitar solo?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, born on July 26, 1943, in Dartford, Kent, is best known as the lead singer and a founding member of The Rolling Stones.

angles.name: Ageless rock icon
angles.copy: Can you believe Mick Jagger is 83 today?! The Rolling Stones frontman continues to defy age with his incredible energy and iconic stage presence. What's your favourite Stones track?

events.title: MTV launched
events.blurb: On August 1, 1981, MTV launched in the United States, revolutionising the music industry with its 24-hour music video programming, famously kicking off with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star'.

angles.name: MTV's ironic debut
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV launched with 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. The irony isn't lost on us, but what an impact it had on music and pop culture! What was the first music video you remember seeing?

events.title: Oasis released Definitely Maybe
events.blurb: Oasis's debut album, 'Definitely Maybe', was released on August 29, 1994, becoming a landmark of Britpop and one of the fastest-selling debut albums in UK history.

angles.name: Britpop masterpiece anniversary
angles.copy: On this day in 1994, Oasis unleashed 'Definitely Maybe' on the world. A quarter-century on, it's still a Britpop masterpiece. What's your favourite track from the album?

events.title: Woodstock Festival took place
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in 1960s counterculture, took place from August 15 to 18, 1969, on Max Yasgur's dairy farm in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Peace, love, and music
angles.copy: 57 years ago today, the Woodstock Festival began – three days of peace, love, and iconic music that defined a generation. Which legendary performance do you wish you'd seen live?

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: Remembering Elvis Presley, the 'King of Rock and Roll', who passed away on August 16, 1977. He would have been 91 this year.

angles.name: Tribute to The King
angles.copy: On this day, we remember the one and only Elvis Presley. His music and legend continue to live on, forever the King of Rock and Roll. What's your favourite Elvis song?

events.title: Tupac Shakur shooting anniversary
events.blurb: On September 7, 1996, rapper Tupac Shakur was shot in a drive-by incident in Las Vegas, tragically leading to his death six days later on September 13. He would have been 55 this year.

angles.name: Remembering Tupac's legacy
angles.copy: Remembering Tupac Shakur today. His impact on music and culture remains profound, a true lyrical genius gone too soon. What's your most powerful Tupac track?

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death
events.blurb: Remembering the unparalleled guitar legend Jimi Hendrix, who tragically passed away on September 18, 1970, at the age of 27. He would have been 84 this year.

angles.name: Jimi Hendrix tribute
angles.copy: On this day, we pay tribute to the incredible Jimi Hendrix. His groundbreaking guitar work changed music forever and continues to inspire generations. What's your favourite Jimi Hendrix track?

events.title: The Beatles released Abbey Road
events.blurb: The Beatles' iconic album 'Abbey Road' was released on September 26, 1969, in the UK, becoming their final recorded album and featuring that legendary zebra crossing cover.

angles.name: Iconic album anniversary
angles.copy: 57 years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. From that unforgettable album cover to timeless tracks, it's a true masterpiece. What's your favourite song from the record?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Christine Ellen Hynde, born on September 7, 1951, in Akron, Ohio, is the founding member, lead vocalist, and primary songwriter of The Pretenders.

angles.name: Pretenders frontwoman's birthday
angles.copy: Happy 75th Birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! The iconic voice of The Pretenders, a true rock legend with an unmistakable style. What's your favourite Pretenders hit?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: William John Paul Gallagher, born on September 21, 1972, in Manchester, England, is best known as the lead singer and co-founder of Oasis.

angles.name: Rock 'n' Roll Star's birthday
angles.copy: Happy 54th Birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! The iconic voice of Oasis and a true rock 'n' roll star. What's your favourite Liam moment, musical or otherwise?

events.title: John Lennon released Imagine (single)
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic single 'Imagine' was released on October 11, 1971, becoming his best-selling solo single and a timeless anthem for peace.

angles.name: Imagine's enduring message
angles.copy: On this day, we celebrate the release of John Lennon's 'Imagine'. Its message of peace and unity resonates just as strongly today as it did in 1971. What does 'Imagine' mean to you?

events.title: Apple released the first iPod
events.blurb: Apple introduced the first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, with the slogan '1,000 songs in your pocket', revolutionising portable music.

angles.name: iPod's 25th anniversary
angles.copy: 25 years ago today, Apple launched the first iPod! '1,000 songs in your pocket' changed everything for music lovers. Did you have an original iPod?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated as James Bond Day, marking the anniversary of the world premiere of the first James Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: 007's enduring legacy
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! From the gadgets to the girls and those iconic theme tunes, 007 remains a global phenomenon. Who's your favourite Bond, and what's the best Bond song?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: Remembering John Lennon, born on October 9, 1940, in Liverpool. The iconic songwriter, musician, and peace activist would have been 86 today.

angles.name: Tribute to a legend
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the visionary John Lennon. His music and message continue to inspire generations worldwide. What's your favourite John Lennon song?
